summary: The PoC demonstrates resource exhaustion vulnerability through unbounded schedule creation. Unlike backup_limit and database_limit, there is no schedule_limit field or validation, enabling unlimited schedule creation.
expected_security_impact: Unauthenticated users with schedule.create permission can create unlimited schedules, exhausting database storage, CPU cycles during schedule processing, and causing denial of service for the entire Panel instance.
actual_observed_result: Code analysis confirmed the vulnerability: ScheduleController::store() creates schedules without limit checks, StoreScheduleRequest has no schedule count validation, and Server model has backup_limit and database_limit fields but NO schedule_limit field. This inconsistency allows unlimited resource consumption.
verification_method: Static code analysis verifying: (1) missing schedule_limit field on Server model, (2) no schedule count validation in ScheduleController, (3) insufficient request validation, (4) comparison with protected resources (backup_limit, database_limit) that ARE enforced.
evidence_produced_by_run: ScheduleController.php:53-77 creates schedules without limit check
StoreScheduleRequest.php has no validation rules for schedule count limits
Server.php model has backup_limit (line 100) and database_limit (line 99) but NO schedule_limit field
No equivalent schedule_limit enforcement found anywhere
limitations_or_blockers: Full runtime reproduction requires: Pterodactyl Panel, database, authentication, server instance
Docker PoC demonstrates vulnerability through code analysis and comparison
Actual mass schedule creation skipped to avoid resource exhaustion in PoC environment

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones pterodactyl/panel, checks out 1.0-develop, mounts at /target-repo
